zh.lifeSummary: 當Egino II. Graf von Freiburg於1250年出生在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀時，他的父親Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach25歲，母親Sophia von Hohenzollern23歲。他於1272年與Katharina von Lichtenberg結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1271年被列為regentschaft als graf von freiburg 。他去世於1317年12月24日在德國巴登福包格享年67歲，埋葬在德國巴登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Egino II. Graf von Freiburg于1250年出生在德国爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀时，他的父亲Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach25岁，母亲Sophia von Hohenzollern23岁。他于1272年与Katharina von Lichtenberg结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1271年被列为regentschaft als graf von freiburg 。他去世于1317年12月24日在德国巴登福包格享年67岁，埋葬在德国巴登。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Freiburg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German Freiburg or Freiberg .
Americanized form of Vreeburg, shortened variant of Vredenburg .

variants.name: Freiberg
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called Freiberg, from Middle High German vrī ‘free, independent’ + berg ‘mountain, hill’ (possibly an altered form of burg ‘castle, fortified town’).

nameMeanings.name: Egino
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the many given names ending in -gino, e.g. Giorgino, pet form of Giorgio , and Luigino, pet form of Luigi .

nameMeanings.name: II.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

en.lifeSummary: When Egino II. Graf von Freiburg was born in 1250, in Urach, Urach, Württemberg, Germany, his father, Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach, was 25 and his mother, Sophia von Hohenzollern, was 23. He married Katharina von Lichtenberg in 1272. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. In 1271, his occupation is listed as regentschaft als graf von freiburg . He died on 24 December 1317, in Freiburg, Baden, Germany, at the age of 67, and was buried in Baden, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Egino II. Graf von Freiburg est né en 1250, à Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Allemagne, son père, Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Sophia von Hohenzollern, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Katharina von Lichtenberg en 1272. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. En 1271, sa profession de son indiquée est regentschaft als graf von freiburg . Il est décédé le 24 décembre 1317, à Freiburg, Baden, Allemagne, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Baden, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Egino II. Graf von Freiburg 1250 in Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach 25 und seine Mutter Sophia von Hohenzollern 23. Er hat 1272 Katharina von Lichtenberg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. 1271 ist sein Beruf als regentschaft als graf von freiburg  aufgeführt. Er starb am 24. Dezember 1317 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Freiburg, Baden, Deutschland und wurde in Baden, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Egino II. Graf von Freiburg è nato il nel 1250 nel Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Germania, il suo padre, Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Sophia von Hohenzollern, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Katharina von Lichtenberg nel 1272. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Nel 1271, la sua occupazione è indicata come regentschaft als graf von freiburg . Morì il 24 dicembre 1317, nel Freiburg, Baden, Germania, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Baden, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Egino II. Graf von Freiburgが1250年にドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト ウラチウラチで生まれた時，父Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urachは25歳，母Sophia von Hohenzollernは23歳であった。1272年にKatharina von Lichtenbergと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1271年，職業はregentschaft als graf von freiburg と記録されている。彼は1317年12月24日にドイツバデンフレイブルグで67歳で死去し，ドイツバデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Egino II. Graf von Freiburg(이)가 1250년 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 라치 라치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Sophia von Hohenzollern(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1272년 Katharina von Lichtenberg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1271년, 그의 직업은 regentschaft als graf von freiburg 이었다. 그는 1317년 12월 24일 독일 바든 프레이버그에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 바든에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Egino II. Graf von Freiburg nasceu em 1250, em Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemanha, seu pai, Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Sophia von Hohenzollern, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Katharina von Lichtenberg em 1272. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Em 1271, sua profissão aparece como regentschaft als graf von freiburg . Ele faleceu em 24 de dezembro de 1317, em Freiburg, Baden, Alemanha, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Baden, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Egino II. Graf von Freiburg родился в 1250 г. в местности урэич, обирамт урэич, вuрттембирг, Германия, его отцу по имени Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Sophia von Hohenzollern, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Katharina von Lichtenberg в 1272 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. В 1271 году он работал по профессии regentschaft als graf von freiburg . Он умер 24 декабря 1317 г. в местности фрибург, баден, Германия в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности баден, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Egino II. Graf von Freiburg nació en 1250, en Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemania, su papá, Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Sophia von Hohenzollern, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Katharina von Lichtenberg en 1272. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. En 1271, su ocupación registrada era regentschaft als graf von freiburg . Murió el 24 de diciembre de 1317, en Freiburg, Baden, Alemania, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Baden, Alemania.
